title: gymnogramma involuta
intro: Gymnogramma involuta is a type of flowering fern, also known as a gymnosperm, which is a plant that produces seeds, but not flowers in the classical sense. Traditionally, the Gymnogramma involuta has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Gymnogramma involuta include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. Gymnogramma involuta is often prepared as a tea or tincture, with some herbalists recommending a dose of 2-3 grams of dried plant material per day, steeped in hot water for 5-7 minutes to release its medicinal properties.
